findings.detail: CONFIRMED: shadow_votes parameter is GONE from generate_decision() signature. Current signature: (l1, l2, l3, red_team, resonance, hypotheses=None, fragility_report=None, cross_border_report=None). Zero occurrences of 'shadow_votes' in entire file (grep verified). All shadow consensus code paths removed. This is the definitive cleanup of the shadow_votes parameter.

PREVIOUSLY REPORTED: _build_hypothesis_summary() embeds LLM-generated text (core_logic, bear_case, hypothesis, layer_N_narrative) from HypothesisResult into decision prompt via f-strings. Upstream LLM output flows into downstream LLM input. MITIGATION: chat_pro() in gateway/async_client.py applies input_guard sanitization to both system and user prompts. Signal conflict text from _detect_signal_conflicts() follows same pattern — Chinese descriptions containing hypothesis text and computed divergence values. This is defense-in-depth (gateway-level) rather than defense-at-source (decision.py). No new injection surfaces introduced.

DECISION_SYSTEM_PROMPT specifies 25% total heat limit and combined stop-losses ≤ 25% total equity, but enforcement is prompt-only. _parse_decision_response() does not programmatically cap position_size_pct. An LLM hallucinated value exceeding 25% would pass through unchecked. gate3_interaction.py pre_trade_checklist provides defense-in-depth with POSITION_WITHIN_LIMIT check.

_parse_decision_response() handles: JSON-in-code-fences, direct JSON, partial JSON (finds {} boundaries), complete parse failure (returns DecisionOutput with error message). All field accessors use .get() with defaults. No eval/exec.
If resonance fails AND no green-light tickers → returns no_trade DecisionOutput without LLM call (line 116-125). Prevents AI hallucination when there's nothing to decide.
Conflicts are flagged for human review with explicit instruction: '请在你的决策中处理这些冲突——不要忽略或平均化，作出判断。' Not auto-resolved — correct safety design.

notes: DEFINITIVE AUDIT for shadow_votes removal. Parameter is GONE — signature cleaned, all code paths removed, zero occurrences in file. Cross-call prompt injection risk (LLM output → LLM input) is a known architectural pattern mitigated by gateway-level input_guard — acknowledged as defense-in-depth rather than defense-at-source. Signal conflict detection is well-designed: additive section, getattr() safety, non-resolution policy.
